chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 5: Project 1 — AI Classification Agent (Agent Pattern)
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the fifth chapter of the IVD book. First hands-on
# project in Part II: build a lead scoring classifier from scratch using IVD,
# apply the agent-classifier recipe, and measure baseline vs IVD.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_2_practice.chapters[0]
# Source:    recipes/agent-classifier.yaml, framework.md, templates/intent.yaml
# Version: 1.3
# Created: 2026-02-09
# Status: Pending
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Guide the reader through building a lead scoring classifier from scratch using IVD—writing the intent artifact, applying the agent-classifier recipe, implementing with an AI assistant, verifying alignment, and measuring baseline vs IVD."
  
  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Has built a complete, working AI classification agent (lead scorer) using IVD
    2. Can apply the agent-classifier recipe to a real problem
    3. Has written a module-level intent artifact for the agent (scope, intent, constraints, verification)
    4. Understands the full IVD flow in practice: describe → AI writes intent → review → AI implements → AI verifies
    5. Has measurable evidence: baseline (no IVD) vs IVD approach (iterations, clarity, maintainability)
    6. Recognizes the pattern so they can apply it to other classification domains (tickets, content, facts)
    
    This is the first practice project. The reader transitions from "I understand IVD" (Part I) to "I can build with IVD." Code and intent artifacts are the centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale.
  
  success_metric: |
    - Reader has a working lead scoring agent at the end of the chapter
    - Reader wrote (or followed along with) a module-level intent artifact referencing the agent-classifier recipe
    - Reader can articulate what the agent-classifier recipe provides and how to customize it
    - Baseline vs IVD measurement is clear: fewer iterations, less drift, verifiable constraints
    - Reader sees the pattern: recipe → intent → implement → verify; ready to apply in Ch6–10
    - Chapter ends with pattern recognition bridge to Project 2 (Chapter 6)
  
  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_2 purpose: build real projects with IVD)"
    - "recipes/agent-classifier.yaml (canonical recipe)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch5)
# ======================================================================

: fewer iterations, less drift, verifiable constraints
    - Reader sees the pattern: recipe → intent → implement → verify; ready to apply in Ch6–10
    - Chapter ends with pattern recognition bridge to Project 2 (Chapter 6)
  
  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_2 purpose: build real projects with IVD)"
    - "recipes/agent-classifier.yaml (canonical recipe)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch5)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "5.1"
      title: "Project Overview: Lead Scoring for Sales Pipeline"
      required_content:
        - "What we're building: an AI agent that classifies sales leads as qualified/not-qualified using LLM-based understanding"
        - "Why this project: relatable business problem; requires context understanding (not keyword matching); clear success metric (accuracy)"
        - "What the reader will learn: the full IVD cycle on a real agent, recipe usage, measurement"
        - "Prerequisites: Part I concepts (intent artifacts, principles, four levels); basic Python; access to an LLM API (OpenAI/Anthropic)"
        - "End state: working agent with intent artifact, tests, and baseline-vs-IVD measurement"
    
    - id: "5.2"
      title: "Step 1: Write the Intent Artifact (Module-Level, including interface and roles)"
      required_content:
        - "Start with natural language: reader describes what they want ('I need an agent that scores leads...')"
        - "AI writes the intent artifact: scope (level=module, type=agent), intent (summary, goal, success_metric), constraints (accuracy >= 80%, latency < 2s, cost tracking)"
        - "Reference the agent-classifier recipe: recipe: 'agent-classifier', recipe_version: '1.0' — show how the recipe provides structure"
        - "parent_intent: link to system intent (or explain that in a real project this would point to the product's system intent)"
        - "Interface section: because this agent exposes callable tools (classify, get_model_info, etc.), add the interface section documenting the agent's tool surface — type: agent, tools with name, description, parameters, returns, test path. First demonstration of this optional section."
        - "Roles section (experimental): if the agent has distinct behavioral modes (e.g., classify mode vs explain mode vs batch mode), add the roles section documenting each mode's constraints, tool subset, and verification. Show how roles complement interface: interface = WHAT the agent can do; roles = HOW it behaves in different contexts. First demonstration of this experimental section."
        - "Human reviews: 'Is this what I meant?' — emphasize Principle 6 (AI proposes, human confirms alignment)"
        - "Show the complete module-level YAML artifact inline (the chapter's primary teaching artifact), including interface and roles sections"
        - "Highlight: the intent is the contract; everything that follows must satisfy these constraints. Interface makes the tool surface part of

enting each mode's constraints, tool subset, and verification. Show how roles complement interface: interface = WHAT the agent can do; roles = HOW it behaves in different contexts. First demonstration of this experimental section."
        - "Human reviews: 'Is this what I meant?' — emphasize Principle 6 (AI proposes, human confirms alignment)"
        - "Show the complete module-level YAML artifact inline (the chapter's primary teaching artifact), including interface and roles sections"
        - "Highlight: the intent is the contract; everything that follows must satisfy these constraints. Interface makes the tool surface part of the contract; roles make behavioral modes part of the contract."
    
    - id: "5.3"
      title: "Step 2: Define Constraints and Success Criteria"
      required_content:
        - "Deep dive into constraints: each constraint has a requirement, test path, and consequence_if_violated (Principle 2)"
        - "classification_accuracy: accuracy >= 0.80; test: tests/test_accuracy.py::test_classification_accuracy"
        - "response_time: p95 latency < 2s; test: tests/test_performance.py::test_latency"
        - "cost_per_classification: average cost < $0.05; test: tests/test_cost.py::test_cost_tracking"
        - "Show how constraints from the recipe template are customized to this specific agent"
        - "Verification plan: how will we know it works? Test cases (happy path, edge case, error handling) from the recipe's verification_pattern"
        - "Emphasize: constraints are not aspirational — they are executable checks (Principle 2: Understanding Must Be Executable)"
    
    - id: "5.4"
      title: "Step 3: Implement with AI Assistant (Claude/GPT)"
      required_content:
        - "The IVD implementation flow: hand the intent artifact to the AI assistant → AI reads the intent → AI implements against constraints"
        - "Show the implementation pattern from the recipe: directory structure (agent/, classifier.py, validator.py, prompts.py, tests/)"
        - "Walk through key implementation decisions: LLM provider, prompt design (few-shot examples), output validation (Pydantic)"
        - "AI implements: show code snippets for the main agent class, classifier, and validator (recipe's code_structure)"
        - "Emphasize: the AI has the intent as context — it knows what to build, what constraints to satisfy, and what tests to write"
        - "What if the AI drifts? Compare AI output against intent constraints; if a constraint is violated, the AI self-corrects (Principle 4: Continuous Verification)"
    
    - id: "5.5"
      title: "Step 4: Verify Alignment (Intent ↔ Code ↔ Interface ↔ Roles)"
      required_content:
        - "Run the constraint tests: accuracy test, latency test, cost test"
        - "Interface verification: do the implemented tool signatures match the interface section? (parameter types, returns, test paths all checkable)"
        - "Roles verification: does the agent respect role constraints? (e.g., explain mode doesn't modify data, batch mode doesn't send individual responses)"
        - "Bidirectional verification (Principle 3): does the code satisfy the intent? Does the intent still

nuous Verification)"
    
    - id: "5.5"
      title: "Step 4: Verify Alignment (Intent ↔ Code ↔ Interface ↔ Roles)"
      required_content:
        - "Run the constraint tests: accuracy test, latency test, cost test"
        - "Interface verification: do the implemented tool signatures match the interface section? (parameter types, returns, test paths all checkable)"
        - "Roles verification: does the agent respect role constraints? (e.g., explain mode doesn't modify data, batch mode doesn't send individual responses)"
        - "Bidirectional verification (Principle 3): does the code satisfy the intent? Does the intent still reflect the actual behavior?"
        - "Show test output: passing tests with actual metrics (accuracy: 0.85, latency p95: 1.2s, cost: $0.03)"
        - "What if a test fails? Walk through a failure scenario and how to iterate (adjust prompt, update constraint, re-verify)"
        - "Continuous verification setup: how to keep these checks running (CI, monitoring dashboard per recipe verification_pattern)"
        - "The verification loop closes: intent → implement → verify → intent is source of truth"
    
    - id: "5.6"
      title: "Results: Baseline (No IVD) vs IVD Approach"
      required_content:
        - "Baseline scenario: build the same agent without IVD — describe what happens (multiple iterations, ambiguous requirements, drift, 'does this work?' uncertainty)"
        - "IVD scenario: what actually happened following the IVD process (clear intent, fewer iterations, verifiable constraints, measurable results)"
        - "Measurement comparison (table or clear list): iterations to working agent, time to first working version, number of constraint violations caught, maintainability (can a new AI session understand the intent?)"
        - "This is the first concrete evidence for the reader — IVD produces measurable improvement"
        - "Tie back to Chapter 1 (alignment crisis) and Chapter 2 (IVD paradigm): 'This is what we meant by verifiable alignment'"
        - "Note: measurements are illustrative from author experience, not controlled experiments (constraint: no_unsourced_statistics)"
    
    - id: "5.7"
      title: "Key Takeaways and Pattern Recognition"
      required_content:
        - "Key Points / Summary block (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
        - "Pattern recognition: what you just did is a repeatable pattern — recipe → intent → implement → verify → measure"
        - "The agent-classifier recipe gave you structure; the intent artifact gave you verifiable constraints; the verification loop gave you confidence"
        - "This pattern applies to any AI agent: ticket router, content classifier, fact checker (recipe examples)"
        - "Bridge to Chapter 6: 'In the next project, you'll tackle a multi-step workflow — a fundamentally different challenge that introduces workflow-level intents'"
        - "Checklist: 'Before you move on' — did you write your intent? Did your constraints pass? Did you measure baseline vs IVD?"
  
  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect bo

traints; the verification loop gave you confidence"
        - "This pattern applies to any AI agent: ticket router, content classifier, fact checker (recipe examples)"
        - "Bridge to Chapter 6: 'In the next project, you'll tackle a multi-step workflow — a fundamentally different challenge that introduces workflow-level intents'"
        - "Checklist: 'Before you move on' — did you write your intent? Did your constraints pass? Did you measure baseline vs IVD?"
  
  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch5 key_points: lead scoring classifier, agent-classifier recipe, baseline vs IVD measurement"
      - "At least one complete module-level YAML intent artifact inline (section 5.2)"
      - "Code snippets for agent implementation (section 5.4) — recipe's code_structure adapted"
      - "Baseline vs IVD comparison table or clear structured comparison (section 5.6)"
      - "1–2 quotable lines where natural (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "Checklist in 5.7 (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Clear bridge to Chapter 6 (workflow pattern)"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent Ch5; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "Build a lead scoring classifier from scratch using IVD — the first complete practice project"
  - "Apply the agent-classifier recipe: recipe provides structure (directory, constraints, verification), intent customizes it to the problem"
  - "Document the agent's tool surface using the interface section — type, tools, parameters, returns, test paths (first demonstration of this optional section)"
  - "Document the agent's behavioral modes using the roles section (experimental) — first demonstration of context-dependent behavior declared in intent"
  - "Full IVD cycle in practice: describe → AI writes intent → human reviews → AI implements → AI verifies against constraints"
  - "Constraints are executable, not aspirational: accuracy >= 80%, latency < 2s, cost < $0.05 — each with a test path (Principle 2)"
  - "Measure: baseline (no IVD) vs IVD approach — fewer iterations, less drift, verifiable alignment"
  - "Pattern recognition: recipe → intent → implement → verify → measure is repeatable for any classification agent"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block and a pre-move-on checklist"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section plus checklist"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; grep 'checklist\\|Before you move on' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"
  
  - name: "quotable_principl

========================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block and a pre-move-on checklist"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section plus checklist"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; grep 'checklist\\|Before you move on' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"
  
  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable, memorable lines in chapter where natural"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is tweetable/memorable"
    test: "At least one bold standalone sentence in chapter.md works as a tweet/pull-quote"
  
  - name: "writing_style_part_2"
    requirement: "Code and intent artifacts are centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale. 'Build this' drives learning."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.code_and_prose.part_2"
    validation: "Manual: code/YAML leads, prose supports; reader is building, not just reading"
    test: "chapter.md contains at least 3 fenced code/YAML blocks; code blocks appear before or alongside explanatory prose, not after long prose-only passages"
  
  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, paragraph structure."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
    test: "Key terms bolded on first use; code font for file paths and commands; no paragraph exceeds 10 sentences without subheading"
  
  - name: "recipe_alignment"
    requirement: "Agent implementation must align with recipes/agent-classifier.yaml structure (directory, constraints_template, implementation_pattern, verification_pattern)"
    source: "recipes/agent-classifier.yaml"
    validation: "Chapter's YAML artifact and code snippets match recipe patterns; no contradictions with recipe"
    test: "Directory structure, constraint names, and verification pattern in chapter.md match recipes/agent-classifier.yaml; diff produces zero contradictions"
  
  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch5 aligns with framework principles and recipe; no contradictions"
    test: "No principle, optional section, or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
  
  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months) unless in examples/code"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding code/examples) returns no matc

nt.yaml"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch5 aligns with framework principles and recipe; no contradictions"
    test: "No principle, optional section, or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
  
  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months) unless in examples/code"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding code/examples) returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline)\\b' chapter.md outside fenced code blocks returns 0 matches"
  
  - name: "no_unsourced_statistics"
    requirement: "Baseline vs IVD measurements must be marked as illustrative (author experience) unless backed by controlled study"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    validation: "Measurement section includes caveat about illustrative data"
    test: "Section 5.6 contains explicit caveat text (e.g., 'illustrative', 'author experience', 'not a controlled study')"
  
  - name: "author_sourced_examples"
    requirement: "The lead scoring scenario and code examples should reflect the author's real experience with the agent-classifier recipe (fact_checker, lead_scorer examples in recipe)"
    source: "recipes/agent-classifier.yaml.examples"
    validation: "Manual: examples feel real, not generic; reference actual recipe examples"
    test: "Lead scoring scenario references recipe examples (fact_checker, lead_scorer); no generic 'widget factory' or 'todo app' examples"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 5 is the first practice project — lead scoring classifier using the agent-classifier recipe. Part II sequence: Ch5 agent (beginner), Ch6 workflow (intermediate), Ch7 infrastructure, Ch8 integration, Ch9 full-stack, Ch10 existing codebase."
  
  reasoning: |
    - Part II purpose: build real projects with IVD; learn by doing. Ch5 is the entry point — beginner complexity, relatable problem.
    - Lead scoring chosen because: (1) relatable business domain, (2) requires LLM-based understanding (not trivial keyword matching), (3) clear success metric (accuracy), (4) recipe already exists and is validated (agent-classifier.yaml).
    - Recipe-first approach: the recipe gives the reader structure; the intent artifact customizes it to the specific problem. This teaches the recipe → intent → implement → verify pattern that repeats in Ch6–10.
    - Baseline vs IVD measurement is critical: this is the first concrete evidence that IVD works. Part III (Evidence) will build on these per-project measurements.
    - Pattern recognition (5.7): the reader should leave seeing the pattern, not just the project. Each subsequent project introduces a new pattern/level while the IVD cycle remains the same.
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_2, Ch5 key_points, placehold

ipe-first approach: the recipe gives the reader structure; the intent artifact customizes it to the specific problem. This teaches the recipe → intent → implement → verify pattern that repeats in Ch6–10.
    - Baseline vs IVD measurement is critical: this is the first concrete evidence that IVD works. Part III (Evidence) will build on these per-project measurements.
    - Pattern recognition (5.7): the reader should leave seeing the pattern, not just the project. Each subsequent project introduces a new pattern/level while the IVD cycle remains the same.
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_2, Ch5 key_points, placeholder); recipes/agent-classifier.yaml (validated recipe with 3+ examples); framework.md (IVD flow)"
  date: "2026-02-09"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Start with a simpler non-AI project (e.g., CLI tool)"
    rejected_because: "Doesn't showcase IVD's AI-era value proposition; the book is about human-AI collaboration. An AI agent is the most compelling first project."
    when_it_works: "If the audience had zero AI experience; our audience is developers already using AI."
  
  - name: "Start with workflow-level (multi-step) project"
    rejected_because: "Too complex for the first project; the reader hasn't practiced the basic IVD cycle yet. Workflow introduces additional concepts (steps, data_flow, error_handling)."
    when_it_works: "If the reader already practiced module-level intents and wants to tackle orchestration."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Reader doesn't have LLM API access (OpenAI/Anthropic) and can't follow along"
    action: "Include a note in 5.1 about prerequisites and alternatives (mock responses, local models); provide example outputs so the chapter works even without API access"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'I couldn't follow along because...'"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condition: "Code examples become stale as LLM APIs evolve"
    action: "Keep code at pattern level (agent, classifier, validator structure); reference the recipe for latest implementation details; avoid pinning to specific API versions in prose"
    monitor: "Recipe version updates; API changelog monitoring"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condition: "Baseline vs IVD comparison feels contrived or cherry-picked"
    action: "Be honest about measurement limitations; mark as illustrative from author experience; show both what went well and what was tricky"
    monitor: "Reviewer feedback on credibility of the comparison"
    severity: "high"
  
  - condition: "Chapter is too long — reader loses momentum on first project"
    action: "Target 25–35 pages; keep code focused on key snippets (not full listing); reference recipe and repo for complete code"
    monitor: "Page count; beta reader completion rate for this chapter"
    severity: "medium"

# IMPLEMENTATION

  status: "pending"
  version: 1

    reference: "book/chapter_writing_process_intent.yaml"
    current_step: "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    completed_steps:

      - "step_2_create_chapter_folder"

    pending_steps:

      - "step_5_cross_check"

      - "step_7_update_book_intent"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent, recipe, and IVD framework)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent Ch5 appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (5.1–5.7) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary block and pre-move-on checklist"
      validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    - check: "At least one complete module-level YAML intent artifact inline (section 5.2)"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[5.2]"
    - check: "Code snippets match recipe implementation_pattern (agent.py, classifier.py, validator.py)"
      validates: "constraints.recipe_alignment"
    - check: "Constraints in the chapter's YAML artifact match recipe constraints_template (accuracy, latency, cost)"
      validates: "constraints.recipe_alignment"
    - check: "Baseline vs IVD comparison present with measurements and caveat about illustrative data"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[5.6], constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    - check: "Writing style: code/YAML centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale (Part II style)"
      validates: "constraints.writing_style_part_2"
    - check: "Bridge to Chapter 6 (workflow pattern) present in 5.7"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[5.7]"
    - check: "No contradictions with ivd_system_intent.yaml, book_system_intent.yaml, or recipes/agent-classifier.yaml"
      validates: "constraints.

      validates: "constraints.master_intent_alignment, constraints.recipe_alignment"
